title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: Boston experienced a 71% rise in needle pickup requests from 2022 to 2025, still 17% below 2020 levels.
Jamaica Plain saw a 149% increase in needle pickup requests from 2020 to 2025, the highest among Boston neighborhoods.
The Back Bay intersection of Blagden and Exeter streets had over 100 needle pickup requests in 2025, up from two in 2020.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo from Scituate, Mass. will run the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo's participation is in honor of her mother, whose Alzheimer’s progression has affected family experiences.
Cimo reflects on cherished memories of her mother's cooking, symbolized by household kitchen items.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins lost 6-3 to the Nashville Predators, falling to 11-14-4 on the road this season.
Boston remains the second Wild Card team in the Eastern Conference, with Columbus just one point behind.
General Manager Don Sweeney contemplates possible trades before the deadline to support a playoff push.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A judge granted Seamus Culleton a stay of removal while his petition for review is resolved, keeping him in Texas custody.
Culleton's deportation is paused for a second time as he seeks to reunite with his U.S. citizen wife from Boston.
Culleton overstayed a visa in 2009; his pending green card and work authorization don't grant legal status, per DHS.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in a house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough.
The fire erupted around 3 a.m.; firefighters from Mansfield and Plainville assisted in extinguishing it.
The fire cause is under investigation; the house sustained serious damage in the blaze.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree coverage expanded by 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, reaching 28.5% excluding Logan Airport.
The University of Vermont conducted the assessment using aerial imagery to inform Boston's climate goals.
Largest canopy gains were in East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury, while Hyde Park and Mattapan saw losses.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group agrees to provide funds for $7.8 million security costs for World Cup events at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough town officials express dissatisfaction, indicating security concerns are not fully addressed despite recent funding commitments.
Foxborough Select Board set a March 17 deadline to decide on granting an event license for the World Cup.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway is closing after nearly a decade, citing rising insurance costs as the primary reason.
The tavern, popular with Northeastern students, was originally named Our House East before becoming Two Saints in 2017.
Managing partner Chuck Hitchcock noted the decline in socializing among younger generations as a factor in the decision to close.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is listed as 'Questionable' for his potential return against the Dallas Mavericks after a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla faces lineup adjustments as Tatum returns, likely impacting players like Sam Hauser and Baylor Scheierman.
Tatum's return expected to alleviate scoring pressure on Jaylen Brown while possibly limiting young guards' minutes in upcoming games.

title: How did Jayson Tatum’s Achilles’ tendon recovery unfold? Recapping the Celtics star’s journey.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to return to the Celtics lineup against the Mavericks after a 10-month recovery from Achilles' surgery.
Tatum underwent successful surgery for a ruptured Achilles' tendon last May and was expected to make a full recovery.
Tatum showed signs of progress throughout his rehab, including attending practice sessions and working out publicly before his return.
